title: Oreobroma yosemitanum
taxonomy: Oreobroma yosemitanum is a species of flowering plant that is part of the family Scrophulariaceae. However, some sources may classify it differently due to ongoing taxonomic revisions. uses: Oreobroma yosemitanum, also known as Yosemites bellflower, has been used in traditional medicine by indigenous communities in North America. intro: Oreobroma yosemitanum, also known as the Yosemite goldfields, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions. Oreobroma yosemitanum contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
